title: Laplacian monomial words
summary: Encodes ambient-Laplacian monomials of the form Δ̃ᵃ(∏ᵢ Δ̃ᵇⁱFᵢ), proves their output homogeneity from explicit algebraic and product-homogeneity interfaces, and specializes the syntax to two and three inputs.

mathematicalContent.heading: A Laplacian word
mathematicalContent.body: A rank-r word records one outer exponent a and one exponent bᵢ for each input. Applied to F=(F₀,…,Fᵣ₋₁), it first differentiates each input, multiplies the results pointwise, and then applies the outer ambient Laplacian power.

mathematicalContent.heading: Total degree and weight
mathematicalContent.body: Every ambient Laplacian lowers homogeneity by two. Hence the word has total Laplacian degree N(W)=a+Σᵢbᵢ and sends input weights (wᵢ) to the expected output weight Σᵢwᵢ−2N(W).

mathematicalContent.heading: What Lean proves
mathematicalContent.body: Assuming the calculus algebraic identities and an explicit interface asserting that a finite product of homogeneous functions has the sum of their weights, Lean proves the input-product weight and then the output weight of the complete word.

mathematicalContent.heading: Low ranks and finite sums
mathematicalContent.body: For two inputs, the constructor (a;b,c) represents Δ̃ᵃ((Δ̃ᵇf)(Δ̃ᶜg)); the three-input constructor stores (a;b,c,d). A finite coefficient table then produces a finite linear combination of rank-two words.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores the outer Laplacian exponent a and the input exponents bᵢ of a rank-r monomial word.
correspondence.leanType: structure LaplacianWord (r : ℕ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Forms the pointwise product of the separately differentiated inputs.
correspondence.leanType: def inputProduct {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Turns a word into the multi-operator obtained by applying the outer Laplacian power to its input product.
correspondence.leanType: def toOperator {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the total number of ambient Laplacians appearing in the word.
correspondence.leanType: def totalPower {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) : ℕ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the predicted output weight by subtracting twice the total Laplacian degree from the sum of the input weights.
correspondence.leanType: def expectedOutputWeight {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (w : Fin r → ℝ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expands the definition of the input product at an ambient point U.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputProduct_apply {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : W.inputProduct CalConf F U = ∏ i : Fin r, CalConf.lapPow (W.inputPower i) (F i) U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expands the operator associated with a single word.
correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : W.toOperator CalConf F = CalConf.lapPow W.outputPower (W.inputProduct CalConf F)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Packages the additional product rule needed here: a pointwise product of homogeneous factors is homogeneous of the sum of their weights.
correspondence.leanType: structure ProductHomogeneityIdentities : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: If each input Fᵢ has weight wᵢ, then its differentiated factor Δ̃ᵇⁱFᵢ has weight wᵢ−2bᵢ, so the input product has the sum of these shifted weights.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputProduct_mapsWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (Hprod : CalConf.ProductHomogeneityIdentities) {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) {w : Fin r → ℝ} {F : CalConf.MultiInput r} (hF : CalConf.IsMultiHomogeneous w F) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (CalConf.totalWeight (fun i : Fin r => w i - 2 * (W.inputPower i : ℝ))) (W.inputProduct CalConf F)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Under the algebraic and product-homogeneity interfaces, the complete Laplacian word maps the prescribed input weights to its expected output weight.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mapsWeights (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (Hprod : CalConf.ProductHomogeneityIdentities) {r : ℕ} (W : LaplacianWord r) (w : Fin r → ℝ) : CalConf.MapsWeights (W.toOperator CalConf) w (W.expectedOutputWeight CalConf w)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Constructs the rank-two word with outer exponent a and input exponents b,c.
correspondence.leanType: def mk (a b c : ℕ) : LaplacianWord 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The outer exponent of the rank-two constructor is a.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_outputPower (a b c : ℕ) : (mk a b c).outputPower = a

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The exponent in the first input slot of W₍a;b,c₎ is b.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_inputPower_zero (a b c : ℕ) : (mk a b c).inputPower 0 = b

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The exponent in the second input slot of W₍a;b,c₎ is c.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_inputPower_one (a b c : ℕ) : (mk a b c).inputPower 1 = c

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For the rank-two word (a;b,c), the total Laplacian degree is a+b+c.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalPower (a b c : ℕ) : (mk a b c).totalPower = a + b + c

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a two-input word, the predicted output weight is w₁+w₂−2(a+b+c).
correspondence.leanType: theorem expectedOutputWeight (a b c : ℕ) (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : (mk a b c).expectedOutputWeight CalConf (CalConf.biWeights w₁ w₂) = w₁ + w₂ - 2 * ((a + b + c : ℕ) : ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expands the rank-two word on the ordered pair of inputs (f,g).
correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_pairInput_apply (a b c : ℕ) (f g : Function Conf) : (mk a b c).toOperator CalConf (CalConf.pairInput f g) = CalConf.lapPow a (fun U => CalConf.lapPow b f U * CalConf.lapPow c g U) := by unfold LaplacianWord.toOperator LaplacianWord.inputProduct mk congr 1 funext U rw [Fin.prod_univ_two] simp [pairInput]

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Constructs the rank-three word with outer exponent a and input exponents b,c,d.
correspondence.leanType: def mk (a b c d : ℕ) : LaplacianWord 3

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The outer exponent of the rank-three constructor is a.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_outputPower (a b c d : ℕ) : (mk a b c d).outputPower = a

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The exponent in the first input slot of W₍a;b,c,d₎ is b.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_inputPower_zero (a b c d : ℕ) : (mk a b c d).inputPower 0 = b

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The exponent in the second input slot of W₍a;b,c,d₎ is c.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_inputPower_one (a b c d : ℕ) : (mk a b c d).inputPower 1 = c

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The exponent in the third input slot of W₍a;b,c,d₎ is d.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mk_inputPower_two (a b c d : ℕ) : (mk a b c d).inputPower 2 = d

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores a finite set S of rank-two Laplacian words together with a real coefficient c_W for every word.
correspondence.leanType: structure BiLaplacianCombinationData

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Associates to finite coefficient data the corresponding finite sum of bidifferential Laplacian words.
correspondence.leanType: def toOperator (D : BiLaplacianCombinationData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.BiOperator

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates the finite word combination pointwise.
correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply (D : BiLaplacianCombinationData) (F : CalConf.BiInput) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : D.toOperator CalConf F U = ∑ W ∈ D.support, D.coeff W * W.toOperator CalConf F U

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The weight theorem is verified only under AlgebraicIdentities and ProductHomogeneityIdentities. The latter is assumed rather than derived here from an Euler-field Leibniz rule. This file does not prove tangentiality, compute boundary defects, establish multilinearity, or show that arbitrary finite combinations share a common output weight.
